verdict_reasoning: C3 is a secreted plasma protein synthesized primarily by the liver and released into circulation. It has no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no outer-leaflet lipidation. Its biological action occurs in the fluid phase or transiently on cell surfaces via covalent thioester-mediated opsonization (C3b deposition onto pathogens/cells). Pre-`no` checklist: (1) No cell-surface-directed antibody/ADC/CAR-T program targets the C3 protein body on the outer leaflet — anti-C3 therapeutics (e.g., AMY-101, APL-2) target the circulating soluble form. (2) C3b does deposit covalently onto cell surfaces via thioester chemistry, but this is deposition onto the extracellular matrix/pathogen/cell coat — C3 itself has no TM partner tethering it stably to the PM inner or outer leaflet. (3) No documented wash-resistant TM-partner anchoring. (4) No documented PM minority pool or cycling. (5) No gene-specific evidence for cancer ecto-display, cell-death surface exposure, germline/developmental restriction, or activation-induced PM translocation. C3b surface opsonization is transient covalent deposition to surface carbohydrates/proteins, not stable anchoring to a TM scaffold.
